i18n_best_practices.overview: Guidelines and best practices for high-quality internationalization and translation work. These practices ensure consistency, accuracy, and maintainability of translation files.

principle.name: accuracy
principle.description: Translations must accurately convey the original meaning

guidelines.guideline: Understand the context before translating
Preserve technical accuracy of domain-specific terms
Maintain the same level of formality as the source
Verify translations make sense in the target language

anti_patterns.pattern: Translating technical terms that should remain in English

principle.name: naturalness
principle.description: Translations should read naturally in the target language

guidelines.guideline: Use natural phrasing for the target language
Adapt idioms rather than translating literally
Consider cultural context and preferences
Ensure proper grammar and sentence structure

example.bad: English: "Sign up now!" → Spanish: "¡Firmar arriba ahora!"
example.explanation: Literal translation sounds unnatural; idiomatic translation flows better

principle.name: consistency
principle.description: Use consistent terminology across all translations

guidelines.guideline: Maintain a glossary of key terms and their translations
Use the same translation for repeated terms
Keep UI element names consistent (buttons, menus, etc.)
Match tone and style across all content

techniques.technique: Create a translation memory or glossary
Review existing translations before adding new ones
Use search_files to find how terms were translated elsewhere

principle.name: completeness
principle.description: Ensure no content is left untranslated

guidelines.guideline: Translate all user-facing strings
Check for placeholder text that needs translation
Verify all nested keys are translated
Include translations for error messages and edge cases

principle.name: naming_conventions
principle.description: Use clear, hierarchical key naming

guidelines.guideline: Use descriptive key names that indicate content
Group related keys under common parent objects
Use camelCase for multi-word keys
Avoid overly generic names like "text1", "label2"

pattern.description: Organize by component or UI element

principle.name: key_synchronization
principle.description: Keep keys synchronized across all language files

guidelines.guideline: All language files should have identical key structures
Add new keys to all languages simultaneously
Remove deprecated keys from all languages
Maintain same nesting depth across languages

verification.check: Compare key counts across language files
Identify orphaned keys not in base language
Find missing keys in any language file

principle.name: deprecation_strategy
principle.description: Handle deprecated or obsolete keys properly

guidelines.guideline: Mark deprecated keys with comments before removing
Remove deprecated keys from all languages at once
Document why keys were removed
Keep a changelog of key changes

principle.name: interpolation
principle.description: Handle variable interpolation correctly

syntax_preservation.rule: Keep variable names exactly as in source

example.issue: Variable names were translated - they must remain unchanged

principle.name: pluralization
principle.description: Handle plural forms according to target language rules

guidelines.guideline: Understand pluralization rules for target language
Use proper plural forms (some languages have multiple)
Maintain pluralization syntax required by i18n library

language.rule: Six forms: zero, one, two, few, many, other

principle.name: html_preservation
principle.description: Preserve HTML tags and structure in translations

guidelines.guideline: Keep all HTML tags intact
Maintain proper tag nesting
Translate only the text content, not tag attributes
Preserve special characters and entities

example.issue: HTML tags were removed - they must be preserved

principle.name: special_characters
principle.description: Handle special characters and escape sequences

guidelines.guideline: Preserve escape sequences (\n, \t, etc.)
Maintain Unicode characters correctly
Keep quotes properly escaped in strings
Preserve special formatting characters

principle.name: localization
principle.description: Adapt content for cultural and regional preferences

aspect.name: date_time
aspect.guideline: Adapt date format mentions to target locale

aspect.name: currency
aspect.guideline: Use appropriate currency symbols and formats
Consider currency placement rules per locale

aspect.name: images_icons
aspect.guideline: Note that some images may need localization

principle.name: cultural_sensitivity
principle.description: Ensure translations are culturally appropriate

guidelines.guideline: Avoid culturally specific idioms that don't translate
Be aware of potentially offensive terms or phrases
Consider formality levels (tu/vous, tú/usted)
Adapt humor carefully or avoid if it doesn't translate

principle.name: right_to_left
principle.description: Consider RTL languages (Arabic, Hebrew)

guidelines.guideline: Maintain logical content order
Keep punctuation appropriate for RTL
Note UI may need RTL-specific styling
Test that translations work in RTL layout

principle.name: structure_preservation
principle.description: Maintain consistent file structure across languages

guidelines.guideline: Keep identical export format in all files
Maintain same ordering of keys when possible
Use consistent indentation and formatting
Keep file headers and comments synchronized

principle.name: version_control
principle.description: Use version control effectively for translations

guidelines.guideline: Commit all language files together
Write clear commit messages explaining changes
Tag releases with translation completeness status
Track translation progress in documentation

principle.name: performance
principle.description: Optimize translation files for performance

guidelines.guideline: Keep translation values concise
Avoid excessive nesting when possible
Consider lazy loading for large translation files
Split very large files by feature/module

principle.name: review_process
principle.description: Establish review procedures for translations

steps.step: Technical review - verify syntax and structure
Context review - ensure translations work in UI

principle.name: testing
principle.description: Test translations in actual application context

checks.check: Verify translations display correctly in UI
Check for text overflow or truncation issues

principle.name: documentation
principle.description: Document translation decisions and context

pitfall.name: over_translation
pitfall.description: Translating things that should remain in original language

examples.example: Technical terms with no good translation

pitfall.solution: Maintain a list of terms that should not be translated

pitfall.name: literal_translation
pitfall.solution: Understand the meaning and intent, then express naturally in target language

pitfall.name: inconsistent_terminology
pitfall.description: Using different translations for the same term
pitfall.solution: Create and maintain a translation glossary

pitfall.name: ignoring_context
pitfall.description: Translating without understanding how text is used
pitfall.solution: Review UI or documentation to understand context before translating

pitfall.name: breaking_syntax
pitfall.description: Accidentally modifying interpolation or HTML syntax
pitfall.solution: Carefully preserve all technical elements; validate file after changes

efficiency_tips.tip: Use search_files to find existing translations of similar terms
Translate related keys together to maintain consistency
Keep a translation glossary document for reference
Use apply_diff for surgical edits to maintain file formatting
Batch similar translation tasks for efficiency
Review entire context before starting translation
